rationale: The citing paper does more than list this reference: it explicitly reports Chernev and Gal's core finding (vice-plus-virtue meals judged as lower in calories than a vice alone) and uses it to define a prior stream on calorie-estimation bias. It then positions its own contribution against that stream, arguing that such perceptual estimation effects do not readily account for the paper's observed consumption effects and goal-conflict mechanism. This gives the citation a clear conceptual positioning role rather than a passing background mention.
relation_description: The citing paper uses Chernev and Gal (2010) as prior evidence on cognitive calorie-estimation biases in vice-virtue combinations, then contrasts that line with its own focus on actual consumption and motivational goal conflict. The citation functions as a substantive interpretive contrast in framing what this paper adds.
